title: Where Super-Resolution Is Used
concept: Super-Resolution powers real-world applications from smartphone camera zoom and satellite/medical imaging enhancement to restoring old media and boosting the input resolution for downstream vision models.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What tradeoff does SRGAN introduce compared to pure pixel/perceptual loss training?

options.text: Sharper, more realistic texture, but a higher risk of hallucinating fabricated detail
Lower resolution outputs but faster training speed
No tradeoff; SRGAN is strictly better in every way

exercises.explain: Yes! Sharper realism comes with hallucination risk.

cards.title: From phone cameras to satellites
cards.body: Super-resolution isn't just a research curiosity — it's quietly running inside your smartphone camera, satellite imaging pipelines, old film restoration tools, and even helping other vision models see small objects more clearly.

cards.title: Real-World SR Applications
cards.body: Super-Resolution applications span: smartphone digital zoom enhancement, satellite/aerial image sharpening, medical scan clarity improvement, historical photo/film restoration, and pre-processing to boost input resolution for downstream detection/recognition models.

cards.title: A better telescope lens for every camera
cards.body: Think of SR as giving every camera a virtual 'better lens' after the fact — you can't add real optical detail that was never captured, but you can sharpen and clarify what's already there using learned patterns from millions of similar images.

cards.title: Satellite imagery for agriculture
cards.body: Agricultural monitoring satellites capture large areas at moderate resolution. Applying SR sharpens crop-row boundaries and small features, helping downstream models (like a crop health classifier) make better decisions from the same raw satellite pass.

cards.title: SR is a helper, not a universal upgrade
cards.body: It's a myth that running SR before every vision task automatically improves accuracy. If a downstream model was trained on real photos, SR's model-generated (sometimes hallucinated) texture can actually introduce artifacts that confuse it — SR must be evaluated task-by-task, not assumed to always help.

cards.title: SR Use Cases by Risk Tolerance
cards.body: Where SR fits along the accuracy-vs-aesthetics spectrum:

left.text:
Phone camera zoom
Old photo/film restoration
Higher tolerance for plausible detail

right.text:
Medical imaging (with caution)
Legal/forensic evidence (very risky)
Requires careful validation before trust

exercises.prompt: Which of these is a legitimate, common real-world use of Super-Resolution?

options.text: Automatically labeling images with class names

exercises.explain: Correct! Digital zoom enhancement is a very common consumer SR application.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Running Super-Resolution before every downstream vision task is guaranteed to improve that task's accuracy.
exercises.explain: False. SR-introduced texture (sometimes hallucinated) can confuse downstream models trained on real photos; effects must be evaluated per task.

exercises.sentence: Because SR can hallucinate plausible-but-fake detail, it carries higher risk in ___-critical applications like legal evidence.
exercises.explain: Precision-critical applications need careful validation before trusting SR-enhanced detail.

exercises.prompt: Why is satellite imagery a good practical use case for Super-Resolution?

options.text: It sharpens crop-row and small-feature boundaries, helping downstream monitoring models see more clearly
It automatically classifies every pixel into weather categories
It eliminates the need for satellites to capture images at all

exercises.explain: Correct! Sharper agricultural imagery aids downstream analysis models.

exercises.prompt: We have completed our study of Super-Resolution — from SRCNN through perceptual loss and SRGAN to real-world deployment. What is the next logical vision application to study?

options.text: Multi-Object Tracking, which follows detected objects consistently across video frames
Restarting the curriculum from Logic Gates and the Perceptron
Deleting all convolutional layers and training a dense network instead
Skipping directly to deployment without learning any more vision tasks

exercises.explain: Exactly! Now that we can sharpen single images, let's track objects consistently across video frames. Next up: Multi-Object Tracking!
